videos.transcript: Think Vegas is just for grown-ups? Think again. We're counting down the 20 absolute best things to do in Las Vegas with kids. From dazzling shows and quirky museums to wild rides and real life aquariums, Sin City has a playful side your whole family will love. And stick around because at the end, we're sharing five awesome kid-friendly restaurants you won't want to miss. Let's kick off the list with a splash, literally. 20. Kowabanga Bay Water Park. When the desert heat hits, cool off at Kowabanga Bay in nearby Henderson. This splashfilled paradise has massive water slides, a lazy river, and kid-friendly splash zones. With colorful cabanas and wave pools, it's perfect for families looking to beat the heat. Whether you're racing down the Zuma Zuma or floating under palm trees, this water park turns up the fun Vegas style. 19. Tournament of Kings at Excalibur. Get ready to eat with your hands and cheer like a knight. Tournament of Kings is a medieval style dinner show with jousting, sword fights, and pyrochnics. Kids love picking a kingdom to support while munching on a hearty feast. It's high energy, super interactive, and one of the most epic shows in town for families with little adventurers. 18. Area 15 and Omega Mart. Step into a world where reality bends. Area 15 is a massive immersive entertainment space. And its star attraction, Omega Mart by Meow Wolf, is a trippy, mind-bending supermarket full of hidden portals and surreal art. Teens and older kids will love the mystery, neon lights, and strange surprises around every corner. This isn't your typical Vegas attraction. It's a playground for the imagination. 17. M&M's Las Vegas or Hershey's Chocolate World. For sweet tothed explorers, a visit to the four-story M&M's Las Vegas or Hershey's chocolate world is a must. Create custom candies, snap selfies with candy mascots, and explore walls of chocolate in every flavor imaginable. Both are right on the strip and make for quick, colorful, and delicious stops. Perfect for a sugar break. 16. Pinball Hall of Fame. Want to introduce your kids to a bit of retro fun? The Pinball Hall of Fame is packed with hundreds of vintage arcade machines and pinball games from every era. It's fun, quirky, and surprisingly affordable. Parents get a blast of nostalgia, and kids can discover what gaming was like before smartphones. This is classic Vegas fun with no screens in sight. 15. Museum of Illusions. Ready to twist your brain and challenge your senses? The Museum of Illusions is full of optical tricks, holograms, and mindbending exhibits that'll leave everyone puzzled in a good way. Kids love the upside down room and vortex tunnel, while parents get a kick out of the photo illusions. It's hands-on, hilarious, and the kind of weird fun that defines Vegas. 14. Big Apple Coaster. Hold on tight. The Big Apple Coaster at New York, New York, zooms over the Las Vegas strip with wild drops and loops that'll thrill older kids and teens. It's one of the only roller coasters right on the strip and features a VR option for an even crazier experience. It's fast, loud, and absolutely unforgettable. Perfect for families with thrillsekers in tow. 13. Neon Museum. Explore the glitz and glow of vintage Las Vegas at the Neon Museum. This outdoor boneyard showcases iconic signs from the city's past, including retro casinos and quirky motel. Guided tours are familyfriendly and offer cool insights into Vegas history. The signs light up at night, turning the whole place into a glowing wonderland. It's nostalgia and neon rolled into one awesome experience. 12. Bellagio Fountain. No Vegas trip is complete without watching the Bellagio fountains dance to music. Every 15 to 30 minutes, jets of water soar sky high in perfect rhythm with everything from opera to pop. Kids are mesmerized. And the best part, it's completely free. Grab a front row spot along the rail and watch their faces light up with every splash and sparkle. 11. Fly over Las Vegas. Soar above American landmarks without leaving the strip. Fly Over Las Vegas is a state-of-the-art flying theater that suspends you in front of a giant spherical screen while wind, mist, and sense add to the realism. It's a breathtaking ride over the Grand Canyon, Iceland, or the Wild West. Totally immersive, surprisingly soothing, and perfect for all ages. 10. Downtown Container Park. This outdoor shopping and entertainment space is built entirely from repurposed shipping containers, and it's way cooler than it sounds. Families love the giant fireb breathing praying mantis at the entrance and the treehouse style playground right in the middle. With boutique shops, fun art, and family-friendly food spots, it's perfect for a relaxed afternoon. Bonus, live music and movie nights often turn this quirky spot into a lively evening hangout. Nine. Arte Museum. This digital art museum is a total sensory trip. Rooms come alive with projections, lights, and soundsscapes inspired by nature and surreal fantasy. It's a calm, colorful escape from the buzz of the strip that kids can't get enough of. From glowing flowers to flowing waterfalls, the exhibits are designed to be immersive and photo worthy. It's one of Vegas's newest and most stunning spots, and it feels like walking through a dream. Eight. The Flamingo Wildlife Habitat. Yes, right in the middle of the strip, you'll find a tropical hideway filled with exotic birds, koiish, turtles, and of course, flamingos. The flamingo wildlife habitat is free to visit and super peaceful with waterfalls, lush greenery, and daily feedings. It's a quiet break from the buzz of the casinos, and a hidden gem that younger kids especially adore. Bring a camera. It's a great place for some colorful family snapshots. Seven. High Roller Observation Wheel. Take sightseeing to the next level, literally. The High Roller is the world's second tallest observation wheel, and the 30inut ride offers stunning panoramic views of the strip and beyond. The climate controlled pods make it comfy year round, and the slow rotation gives kids time to explore and enjoy the view. Day or night, it's a beautiful and relaxing way to see the city from above. Parents, it's a great photo op, too. Six. Springs Preserve. A lush escape from the neon. Springs Preserve is a nature and science wonderland perfect for curious kids. With interactive museums, botanical gardens, animal exhibits, and even a mini train ride, there's plenty to explore. The preserve also tells the story of Las Vegas's natural history, making it both fun and educational. It's a calm, shady retreat ideal for families who want something slower paced and beautifully grounded in nature. Five. Discovery Children's Museum. This three-story wonderland is every curious kid's dream. From a mini city where they can play pretend to interactive science experiments and art zones, there's something for every age and interest. Kids are drawn to the waterplay area and climbing towers. While parents appreciate the educational twist. It's hands-on, engaging, and easily one of the most well-rounded indoor attractions for families in all of Las Vegas. Four. Red Rock Canyon National Conservation Area. Just a short drive from the strip, Red Rock Canyon offers jaw-dropping desert views, and plenty of space to roam. Enjoy scenic drives, gentle hiking trails, and rock formations that feel straight out of a movie. It's a peaceful escape packed with photo ops and outdoor adventure. Whether you're climbing, strolling, or simply staring at the red cliffs, this is Vegas nature at its finest. Three. Belio Conservatory and Botanical Garden. This everchanging garden is an explosion of color and creativity right in the middle of a luxury resort. The themes change seasonally. Think giant floral animals, whimsical lanterns, and dazzling displays that feel like walking through a fairy tale. It's free, airond conditioned, and a calm moment of beauty that both kids and adults totally get into. Make sure your phone's charged. This place is a photo paradise. Two. Adventure Dome at Circus Circus. Indoor amusement park. Yes, please. The Adventure Dome packs roller coasters, bumper cars, laser tag, and arcade games into a 5acre dome of non-stop action. There's something for every thrill level. From toddler rides to gravitydeying loops. The best part, it's weatherproof, so it's fun year round. Families who crave variety, excitement, and some good old-fashioned chaos are all about this Vegas classic. Number one, Shark Reef Aquarium at Mandandalay Bay. Topping the list is an underwater journey right in the heart of the desert. The Shark Reef Aquarium is home to sharks, rays, sea turtles, and even a golden crocodile. The 360° tunnel lets kids feel like they're swimming with the creatures. It's immersive, a little mysterious, and totally captivating. For a city that never sleeps, this spot offers a surprisingly peaceful deep sea adventure the whole family will remember. After all that exploring, you've probably worked up an appetite. Don't worry, we've got you covered. Rainforest Cafe. Jungle vibes, animatronic animals, and indoor thunderstorms make this spot unforgettable for kids. The themed menu has plenty of familiar favorites, and the wild setting keeps little ones entertained the whole meal. It's dinner with a side of adventure right on the strip. Honey Salt. This cozy off-strip gem serves farm-to-table food with a thoughtful kids menu. Dishes have playful names, and the welcoming atmosphere makes it a peaceful break from the bustle. It's a perfect spot for a family meal that's both wholesome and delicious. Sugar Factory. With giant milkshakes, rainbow colored desserts, and candy packed drinks, this place is sugar heaven. Kids go wild for the over-the-top sweets and fun decor. If you're in the mood to treat your family to something outrageous, this spot brings the sugarfueled excitement, Mr. Barbecue. At Mr. Barbecue, the fun is in the grillityour yourself experience. The vibrant decor, playful atmosphere, and friendly staff make it a great introduction to Korean barbecue for kids. Everyone gets involved and everyone leaves full and happy. A delicious way to bond over fire and food. Carmines. Serving massive portions of Italian comfort food. Carmines is made for sharing. The family style menu is perfect for groups and the laid-back vibe makes it easy with kids in tow. Hearty pasta, juicy meatballs, and a welcoming staff make this a surefire hit. If this video helped you plan your Vegas adventure and you're looking for more familyfriendly fun, check out our video on 16 amazing things to do in Los Angeles with kids. And hey, don't forget to like, subscribe, and drop a comment below telling us your favorite kid-friendly activity in Las Vegas.

The street performers are wild, and if you want old school Vegas vibes, this is where you'll find them. Number six, High Roller Observation Wheel. Think of it as Vegas's version of the London Eye, except instead of historic views, you get a 360° look at the city's casinos, hotels, and desert backdrop. At 550 ft tall, the High Roller is one of the tallest observation wheels in the world. Bonus tip, book a happy halfhour cabin where the bar is inside the ride. Yeah, it's a ferris wheel with drinks. Number five, Caesar's Palace and the Forum Shops. A Vegas classic, Caesar's Palace is home to high-end shopping, jaw-dropping architecture, and of course, that fountain from the hangover. The Forum Shops look like an ancient Roman city, except with luxury brands and talking statues. Whether you're there to shop or just pretend you can afford it, it's a must visit. Number four, Bellagio Mountains and Conservatory. If there's one free attraction you absolutely can miss, it's the Bellagio Fountains. These water shows are choreographed to music and somehow a bunch of water jets and lights can make you emotional. Inside the Bellagio Conservatory is equally stunning with elaborate seasonal displays that make you forget you're in a casino. Number three, the Venetian and Gondelola rides. Why fly to Italy when you can just Vegas it? The Venetian is straight up Venice, but indoors with canals, singing gondelers, and painted sky ceilings that convince your brain it's daytime, even when it's midnight. Take a gondola ride, do some shopping, or just wander around and admire the ridiculously detailed architecture. Number two, the Welcome to Las Vegas sign. Okay, it's just a sign, but it's also the sign. The welcome to fabulous Las Vegas sign is a historic landmark. And if you don't get a photo here, did you even go to Vegas? Pro tip, go early in the morning unless you enjoy waiting in line with about a 100 other tourists. Number one, the Las Vegas Strip. And the most iconic thing to do in Vegas, just walk the strip. The Las Vegas Strip is an entertainment overload with massive hotels, wild attractions, endless night life, and enough flashing lights to power a small country. You'll see everything from fireb breathing volcanoes at the Mirage to a replica Eiffel Tower to street performers dressed as Elvis. Whether you're here to party, sightsee, or just marvel at the madness, the strip is where Vegas really happens. And there you have it, the top 10 things to do in Las Vegas. Whether you're here for the history, the thrills, or just to see how much of your money you can lose, one thing's for sure, Vegas never disappoints.
